text: Writing clean code is like writing a clear message that anyone can understand. The most important rule is to make your code readable for others and for your future self. Use good naming conventions: give variables and functions meaningful names that describe what they do. Instead of "x" or "data," use "userEmail" or "calculateTotal." Keep your functions short and focused on one task. This makes testing and fixing problems much easier. When you write clean code, you improve maintainability, meaning other developers can quickly understand and update your work without confusion. Remember, clean code saves time and reduces errors in the long run.
